llm_operation.reason: 原始测试用例仅提供input shape和dtype，但NLLLoss和CategoricalCrossentropy均为损失函数，必须同时接收预测值（logits/log-probs）和真实标签（target/labels）才能执行forward/call。PyTorch的NLLLoss要求输入为log-probabilities（通常来自log_softmax）和整数类索引target；TensorFlow的CategoricalCrossentropy expects one-hot encoded labels and logits (not probabilities), and by default from_logits=False, so inputs should be probabilities — but commonly used with from_logits=True. Moreover, NLLLoss expects target of shape (N,) with int64 dtype, while CategoricalCrossentropy expects y_true of shape (N, C) for one-hot labels. To enable cross-framework comparison, we must provide both prediction and label inputs consistently. Since the original case lacks any label input and misuses 'input' as sole tensor, it fails with missing argument errors. Repair: add proper 'pred' and 'target' (or 'y_true') fields aligned with each API's signature and semantics — specifically, for NLLLoss: pred=(2,3) log-probs, target=(2,) long; for CategoricalCrossentropy: y_pred=(2,3) logits (with from_logits=True) and y_true=(2,3) one-hot.
